title: Volume Matcher — Activity 6.8 Extension (NCERT Grade 6 Science Ch. 6)
instructions_for_student: Drag each bottle to the tumbler that just fits its volume. On mobile, tap a bottle first then tap a tumbler. Read the reason after each correct match!

additionalProps.finalMessage: Volume is the space a liquid takes up, measured in litres (L) and millilitres (mL). 1 L = 1000 mL. Always check the label on a bottle to know its volume! The SI unit for volume is the cubic metre (m³), where 1 m³ = 1000 L.

agent_readme.description: A three-to-three drag-and-match interactive tool for teaching volume units (mL and L) from NCERT Grade 6 Science Chapter 6.3.6 (Space and volume). Three labelled Indian drinking-water bottles (200 mL, 500 mL, 1 L) must be matched to three tumblers (small glass, medium mug, big jug) of the correct capacity. Correct matches lock with a one-line reason; wrong matches shake and return with a hint. A final card summarises the volume concept.
agent_readme.curriculum_link: NCERT Grade 6 Science — Chapter 6.3.6: Space and Volume / Section 6.4: What is Matter?
key_concepts_taught: Volume is the space occupied by matter (liquid in this case)
Units: litre (L) and millilitre (mL); 1 L = 1000 mL
SI unit for volume: cubic metre (m³); 1 m³ = 1000 L

interaction_modes.drag_and_drop: Desktop — drag a bottle card onto a tumbler drop zone
interaction_modes.tap_to_select_then_tap: Mobile / touch — tap a bottle (highlighted orange border), then tap target tumbler

match_data.label: 200 mL
match_data.reason: 200 mL is a small sip — it fills a small glass exactly. Remember: 1 L = 1000 mL, so 200 mL is just 1/5 of a litre.
match_data.hint: The smallest bottle needs the smallest container! Which tumbler looks like a tiny drinking glass?

match_data.label: 500 mL
match_data.reason: 500 mL = half a litre. It fills a medium mug perfectly — exactly halfway to 1 litre.
match_data.hint: 500 mL is half of 1 litre. Which tumbler is medium-sized — not tiny, not huge?

match_data.label: 1 L
match_data.reason: 1 L = 1000 mL. A big jug holds exactly 1 litre — the largest standard measure. 1 m³ = 1000 L!
match_data.hint: One full litre is a lot of liquid. You need the biggest container of all three!

finalMessage.description: Text shown on the completion overlay summarising the volume concept. Defaults to the standard NCERT-aligned summary if omitted.

default.description: Use built-in match data and default final message by omitting additionalProps

additionalProps.finalMessage: Volume tells us how much space a liquid takes up. Bottles always display their volume in mL or L — read the label before you pour!

scoring.score_strip: Live matched count + animated progress bar (e.g. '2 / 3 matched')
scoring.correct_match: Tumbler locks with coloured fill + bottle badge; success reason shown in green banner
scoring.wrong_match: Tumbler shakes, bottle returns to tray, hint shown in red banner, 1.6 s cooldown
scoring.completion: Fixed full-screen overlay with 3 stars, score, volume fact card, unit badges, Try Again button

decision_tree.student_asks_about_volume_units: Use default tool — all three units (200 mL, 500 mL, 1 L) are covered
decision_tree.student_asks_what_mL_means: Use default tool; the reason card for 200 mL explicitly defines the L↔mL relationship
decision_tree.student_asks_about_bottle_labels: Use default tool; the completion card reinforces reading bottle labels
decision_tree.quick_warm_up_before_lesson: Use default — 3 matches complete in under 2 minutes
